sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 17.55 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.17)

statement.latex: Does there exist an absolute constant$k$such that for any prefrattini subgroup$H$in any finite soluble group$G$there exist$k$conjugates of$H$whose intersection is~$(G)$? { S.\,F.\,Kamornikov} Yes, such a constant exists and is equal to 3 (S.\,F.\,Kamornikov, Int. J. Group Theory, { 6}, no.\,2 (2017), 1--5).

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory IV_PROBLEM_UNDEFINED; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
